response.justification: The candidate is an exceptionally strong fit for the role. As a Staff Engineer at Google and former Senior Engineer at Meta, he possesses direct experience in the exact domain required: building internal automation and AI-agent tooling. His technical skills align very well with the primary stack, featuring extensive experience with TypeScript, Node.js, React, and cloud technologies. While he doesn't explicitly list Nest.js or Vitest, his seniority and background at top-tier companies suggest he can learn new frameworks quickly. His overall experience and domain alignment make him a top-tier candidate.
strengths: Direct experience building automation and AI agent tooling at top-tier companies (Google, Meta).
Deep, long-term experience with the core stack: TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS, and Docker.
Proven seniority (Staff Engineer) and a 'software craftsman' mindset focused on quality and testing.
concerns: No explicit experience listed with Nest.js or Vitest, which are part of the primary stack.
The resume has a confusing entry with a future start date at Google (April 2025), which needs clarification.
His Staff Engineer title may indicate he is seeking a role with a higher level of responsibility or compensation than this position offers.

key_factors.factor: Domain experience in automation/AI tooling at Google/Meta
Extensive experience with core stack (TS, Node, React)
